title: taxus brevifolia subsp. polychaeta
intro: Taxus brevifolia subsp. polychaeta, also known as Pacific Yew, is a subspecies of the Western Yew tree, native to the Pacific coastal regions of North America. The plant has been found to have potential medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit cancer cell growth, making it a subject of interest in cancer research. The health benefits of Taxus brevifolia subsp. polychaeta include its potential anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The plant's extracts and alkaloids are often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures and capsules, although its use is heavily regulated due to concerns over toxicity and the risk of death from ingesting even small amounts.
